{{Infobox location
| name = Iron Hive
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[Iron Hive Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron]] [[Hive]] is a massive, bulbous structure serving as a central hub for the [[Iron Legion]]'s fleet. These entities are composed of fused metal from various crashed starships and can reach the size of a small moon or even a planet. They lack defensive shields to facilitate the rapid deployment and retrieval of fighter craft, making them vulnerable to direct energy attacks. The [[Hives]] function as biological and mechanical nurseries; they are capable of producing [[Iron King]] leaders and are populated by various soldiers, such as [[Pawns]]. While some [[Hives]] operate under the coordination of a [[Bishop]] to manage magical protections, those without such leadership act independently like wild animals, seeking out nearby planets or colonies to consume and infest. The most significant of these structures is the [[Grand Hive]], which has been described as a planet-sized skull made of black iron and white skin. This particular [[Hive]] served as the fortress for the last [[Iron King]] and has been subject to various fates, including being hollowed out, destroyed, or left as smoldering debris.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Constructed from the metal of crashed starships, the [[Iron]] [[Hive]] is a shifting environment that can be destabilized by large weapons and mapped using holograms. During an invasion of the location, the narrator fought through its interior to destroy the [[Iron King]], an act that resulted in the total destruction of the [[Hive]]. This battle was intense enough to leave the narrator's plasma cannons warped. Beyond being a specific location, an [[Iron]] [[Hive]] is also recognized as a type of entity or organization for which one might serve as a [[Shepherd]].}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The narrator's plasma cannons were warped after a battle at the [[Iron Hive]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c6">''The Captain'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Hive]] is a shifting environment that can be destabilized by large weapons.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c17">''The Captain'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Hive]] is a location that can be mapped via hologram.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18">''The Captain'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The narrator fought through the [[Iron Hive]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Hive]] was made from the metal of crashed starships.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c19">''The Captain'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Hive]] was destroyed.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The narrator invaded the [[Iron Hive]] and destroyed the [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c21">''The Captain'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|An [[Iron Hive]] is a type of entity or organization that one might become a [[Shepherd]] for.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c36">''The Captain'', chapter 36</ref>}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The narrator can direct enough [[Aether]] to destroy an [[Iron Hive]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c38">''The Engineer'', chapter 38</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|An [[Iron Hive]] is a type of structure associated with the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c9">''The Knight'', chapter 9</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|An [[Iron Hive]] can float near an inhabited planet.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39">''The Pilot'', chapter 39</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon locations]]
[[Category:Structures]]
